message:
test(fcp-core): pin ApprovalMode + ApprovalScope serde tag matrix (flywheel_connectors-qnll1)

Bead asks for ApprovalReason variant Display + serde tag. fcp-core
has no type literally named ApprovalReason; the approval-classifier
surface that names "why approval is required" splits across:
- ApprovalMode (protocol.rs:1897) — 4 variants
  (None/Policy/Interactive/ElevationToken) carrying serde
  rename_all snake_case. Closest "ApprovalReason" analogue, wired
  into OperationMeta.requires_approval.
- ApprovalScope (provenance.rs:824) — 3 variants
  (Elevation/Declassification/Execution) with internally-tagged
  form (#[serde(tag = "type", rename_all = "snake_case")]) and
  nested struct payloads.

Neither implements Display — pinning targets the serde wire form
plus tag-discriminator presence in CBOR.

Coverage:
- ApprovalMode per-variant JSON tag pinned
  (none/policy/interactive/elevation_token) with audit-log
  regression sentinel
- ApprovalMode JSON + CBOR round-trip per variant; CBOR encodes
  as Text (cross-language consumers)
- ElevationToken uses underscore not hyphen or camelCase
  (elevationToken/elevation-token rejected)
- PascalCase + unknown + camelCase + kebab-case + empty rejected
- ApprovalMode::None token does NOT alias serde null (explicit
  "none" string required)
- 4-variant count + pairwise distinctness
- ApprovalScope per-variant `type` tag pinned (declassification /
  execution) with internally-tagged form flattening payload fields
- ApprovalScope JSON round-trip preserves variant + nested payload
- ApprovalScope CBOR shape: `type` discriminator present in
  encoded map (Value-inspection workaround for the known
  Content-shim quirk on internally-tagged enums with
  hex_or_bytes-encoded ObjectId fields)
- ApprovalScope rejects PascalCase + unknown `type` tag

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
